steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Both authors discuss Nowak and colleagues' study design. The question asks what both authors would agree on — so the answer must be supported by Text 1 AND Text 2.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Excluding root-to-root transfer of nutrients between plants is sufficient to ensure that any observed nutrient transfer must involve a CMN.: Text 2 explicitly rejects this — it argues the barrier's permeability to liquids means a CMN cannot be confirmed as the cause. ✗
  • Choice B — A barrier that is impervious to both roots and fungal strands is necessary to evaluate nutrient transfer via a CMN.: Neither text calls for this. Text 1 says strands were allowed through; Text 2 never suggests blocking strands. ✗
  • Choice C — Zhou and colleagues' study did not find convincing evidence of nutrient transfer between individual plants.: Text 1 says Zhou's study lacked a key methodological step, not that it found no evidence. Text 2 doesn't mention Zhou at all. ✗
  • Choice D — The barriers used in Nowak and colleagues' study effectively excluded root-to-root transmission of nutrients.: Text 1 says the barriers kept root systems separate, excluding root-to-root pathways. Text 2 confirms “separating the plants’ root systems (thereby excluding root-to-root transmission).” ✓
D is correct.
